first_paragraph: **The Most Gallant Order of Pahlawan Negara Brunei**[[1]](#cite_note-1) ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Darjah Pahlawan Negara Brunei Yang Amat Perkasa*), also translated as **The Most Gallant Order of the Hero of the State of Brunei**,[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) is an [order](/wiki/Order_(decoration) "Order (decoration)") of [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"). It was established on 28 November 1959 by [Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Sultan_Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III").[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Order of Brunei

| Order of Pahlawan Negara Brunei | |
| --- | --- |
| Awarded by the [Sultan of Brunei](/wiki/Sultan_of_Brunei "Sultan of Brunei") | |
| Type | [Military decoration](/wiki/Military_decoration "Military decoration") |
| Established | 1959 |
| Country | [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei") |
| Awarded for | For displaying particularly valiant behaviour |
| Status | Currently constituted |
| Sovereign | [Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Hassanal_Bolkiah "Hassanal Bolkiah") |
| Grades | * First Class (PSPNB) * Second Class (DHPNB) * Third Class (PNB) * Fourth Class (PJP) |
| Precedence | |
| Next (higher) | [Order of Paduka Keberanian Laila Terbilang](/wiki/Order_of_Paduka_Keberanian_Laila_Terbilang "Order of Paduka Keberanian Laila Terbilang") |

**The Most Gallant Order of Pahlawan Negara Brunei**[[1]](#cite_note-1) ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Darjah Pahlawan Negara Brunei Yang Amat Perkasa*), also translated as **The Most Gallant Order of the Hero of the State of Brunei**,[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) is an [order](/wiki/Order_(decoration) "Order (decoration)") of [Brunei](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"). It was established on 28 November 1959 by [Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Sultan_Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III").[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

## Current classes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Pahlawan_Negara_Brunei&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Current classes")]

The four classes of appointment to the Order are, from highest grade to lowest grade:

| Class | [Post-nominal](/wiki/Post-nominal_letters "Post-nominal letters") | [Title](/wiki/Malay_styles_and_titles "Malay styles and titles") | [Ribbon bar](/wiki/Ribbon_bar "Ribbon bar") |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| First Class | PSPNB | Dato Seri Pahlawan |  |
| Second Class | DHPNB | Dato Hamzah Pahlawan |  |
| Third Class | PNB | – |  |
| Fourth Class | PJP |  |

## Description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Pahlawan_Negara_Brunei&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Description")]

In the numerous links of the collar, crossed daggers, swords, tridents, and arrows are created in a military-appropriate fashion. Red, green, and white enamel is used to embellish each of these emblems.[[3]](#cite_note-:4-3) The first class's items included a collar chain that measured about 920 millimetres (36 in) and had 16 medallions on it, including two crossed [sabres](/wiki/Sabre "Sabre"), two crossed [tridents](/wiki/Trident "Trident"), and two crossed [lances](/wiki/Lance "Lance"). There were also eight smaller medallions of crossed daggers with different designs made of silver, gilt, and enamel; an adornment or sash badge measuring 65 millimetres (2.6 in) and a star measuring 88 millimetres (3.5 in) that was made of silver, gilt, and enamel, with a [sash](/wiki/Sash "Sash") [riband](/wiki/Ribbon "Ribbon").[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Recipients

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Pahlawan_Negara_Brunei&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Recipients")]

Members of the [armed forces](/wiki/Military "Military") who have displayed particularly valiant behavior are given this order.[[3]](#cite_note-:4-3)

### First Class

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Pahlawan_Negara_Brunei&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: First Class")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/37/Sulaiman_Alidin%2C_deputy_police_commissioner_of_Brunei.jpg/250px-Sulaiman_Alidin%2C_deputy_police_commissioner_of_Brunei.jpg)](/wiki/File:Sulaiman_Alidin,_deputy_police_commissioner_of_Brunei.jpg)

Deputy Police Commissioner Sulaiman wearing his [sash](/wiki/Sash "Sash") in 2024

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c7/Alirupendi_Perudin_-_53856840547.jpg/250px-Alirupendi_Perudin_-_53856840547.jpg)](/wiki/File:Alirupendi_Perudin_-_53856840547.jpg)

Alirupendi wearing his sash in 2024

* Unknown – Field Marshal [Hassanal Bolkiah](/wiki/Hassanal_Bolkiah "Hassanal Bolkiah") – [Sultan of Brunei](/wiki/Sultan_of_Brunei "Sultan of Brunei")[[5]](#cite_note-:5-5)
* Unknown – General [Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Omar Ali Saifuddien III") – Sultan of Brunei[[5]](#cite_note-:5-5)
* Unknown – Major General [Husin](/wiki/Husin_Ahmad "Husin Ahmad") – [Commander of the Royal Brunei Armed Forces](/wiki/Commander_of_the_Royal_Brunei_Armed_Forces "Commander of the Royal Brunei Armed Forces")[[6]](#cite_note-:03-6)
* Unknown – Major General [Ibnu](/wiki/Ibnu_Basit_Apong "Ibnu Basit Apong") – [Deputy Minister of Defence](/wiki/Ministry_of_Defence_(Brunei) "Ministry of Defence (Brunei)")[[7]](#cite_note-:2-7)
* Unknown – Colonel [Kefli](/wiki/Kefli_Razali "Kefli Razali") – Commander of the Royal Brunei Navy[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* Unknown – [George Edwin Coster](/wiki/George_Edwin_Coster "George Edwin Coster") – Assistant Commissioner in the Special Branch[[9]](#cite_note-9)
* 1971 – Lieutenant Colonel [John Simpson](/wiki/John_Simpson_(British_Army_officer) "John Simpson (British Army officer)") – Commander of the [Royal Brunei Malay Regiment](/wiki/Royal_Brunei_Malay_Regiment "Royal Brunei Malay Regiment")[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* 1971 – [James Richard Henry Burns](/wiki/James_Richard_Henry_Burns "James Richard Henry Burns") – [Commissioner of Police](/wiki/Police_commissioner "Police commissioner")[[11]](#cite_note-11)
* 1976 – [Pengiran Umar](/wiki/Pengiran_Umar "Pengiran Umar") – Commissioner of Police[[12]](#cite_note-:9-12)
* 1976 – Lieutenant Colonel [Pengiran Ibnu Basit](/wiki/Pengiran_Ibnu_Basit "Pengiran Ibnu Basit") – Formation of RBLF Second Battalion[[12]](#cite_note-:9-12)
* 1976 – Lieutenant Colonel [Mohammad](/wiki/Mohammad_bin_Daud "Mohammad bin Daud") – Member of [Legislative Council](/wiki/Legislative_Council_of_Brunei "Legislative Council of Brunei")[[12]](#cite_note-:9-12)
* 2005 – [Mohd Bakri Omar](/wiki/Mohd_Bakri_Omar "Mohd Bakri Omar") – [Inspector-General of Police](/wiki/Inspector-General_of_Police_(Malaysia) "Inspector-General of Police (Malaysia)")[[13]](#cite_note-13)
* 2008 – Police-General [Sutanto](/wiki/Sutanto "Sutanto") – [Chief of the Indonesian National Police](/wiki/Chief_of_the_Indonesian_National_Police "Chief of the Indonesian National Police")[[14]](#cite_note-14)
* 2008 – [Musa Hassan](/wiki/Musa_Hassan "Musa Hassan") – [Inspector-General of Police](/wiki/Inspector-General_of_Police_(Malaysia) "Inspector-General of Police (Malaysia)")[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* 2011 – First Admiral [Abdul Aziz](/wiki/Abdul_Aziz_Mohd_Tamit "Abdul Aziz Mohd Tamit") – Commander of Joint Force Headquarters[[16]](#cite_note-:7-16)
* 2011 – First Admiral [Abdul Halim](/wiki/Abdul_Halim_Mohd_Hanifah "Abdul Halim Mohd Hanifah") – Commander of the Royal Brunei Navy[[16]](#cite_note-:7-16)
* 2011 – Brigadier General Yusof – Commander of the [Royal Brunei Land Forces](/wiki/Royal_Brunei_Land_Forces "Royal Brunei Land Forces")[[16]](#cite_note-:7-16)
* 2011 – Brigadier General Jofri – Commander of the [Royal Brunei Air Force](/wiki/Royal_Brunei_Air_Force "Royal Brunei Air Force")[[16]](#cite_note-:7-16)
* 2011 – Major General [Tawih](/wiki/Mohammad_Tawih "Mohammad Tawih") – [Commander of the Royal Brunei Armed Forces](/wiki/Commander_of_the_Royal_Brunei_Armed_Forces "Commander of the Royal Brunei Armed Forces")[[16]](#cite_note-:7-16)
* 2012 – Bahrin Mohd Noor – Deputy Commissioner of Police[[17]](#cite_note-17)
* 2014 – Brigadier General [Wardi](/wiki/Wardi_Abdul_Latip "Wardi Abdul Latip") – Commander of the Royal Brunei Air Force[[18]](#cite_note-18)
* 2015 – Major General [Pengiran Aminan](/wiki/Pengiran_Aminan "Pengiran Aminan") – [Commander of the Royal Brunei Armed Forces](/wiki/Commander_of_the_Royal_Brunei_Armed_Forces "Commander of the Royal Brunei Armed Forces")[[19]](#cite_note-19)
* 2015 – Major General [Hamzah](/wiki/Hamzah_Sahat "Hamzah Sahat") – Commander of the Royal Brunei Armed Forces[[20]](#cite_note-rssing-20)
* 2015 – First Admiral [Pengiran Norazmi](/wiki/Pengiran_Norazmi "Pengiran Norazmi") – Commander of the Royal Brunei Navy[[21]](#cite_note-:05-21)
* 2015 – [Jammy Shah Al-Islam](/wiki/Jammy_Shah_Al-Islam "Jammy Shah Al-Islam") – Commissioner of Police*[[22]](#cite_note-22)*
* 2016 – Brigadier General [Shahril Anwar](/wiki/Shahril_Anwar "Shahril Anwar") – Commander of the Royal Brunei Air Force[[23]](#cite_note-23)
* 2018 – [Irwan Hambali](/wiki/Irwan_Hambali "Irwan Hambali") – [Commissioner of Police](/wiki/Royal_Brunei_Police_Force "Royal Brunei Police Force")[[24]](#cite_note-:1-24)
* 2018 – Brigadier General [Khairul](/wiki/Khairul_Hamed "Khairul Hamed") – Commander of the Royal Brunei Land Forces[[24]](#cite_note-:1-24)
* 2019 – Brigadier General [Sharif](/wiki/Mohammad_Sharif_Ibrahim "Mohammad Sharif Ibrahim") – Commander of the Royal Brunei Air Force[[25]](#cite_note-25)
* 2019 – First Admiral [Othman](/wiki/Othman_Suhaili "Othman Suhaili") – Commander of the Royal Brunei Navy[[26]](#cite_note-:3-26)
* 2021 – Major General [Haszaimi](/wiki/Haszaimi_Bol_Hassan "Haszaimi Bol Hassan") – Commander of the Royal Brunei Armed Forces[[27]](#cite_note-:12-27)
* 2021 – First Admiral [Spry](/wiki/Spry_Serudi "Spry Serudi") – Commander of the Royal Brunei Navy[[28]](#cite_note-:06-28)
* 2021 – Brigadier General [Abdul Razak](/wiki/Abdul_Razak_Abdul_Kadir "Abdul Razak Abdul Kadir") – [Deputy Minister of Defence](/wiki/Ministry_of_Defence_(Brunei) "Ministry of Defence (Brunei)")[[29]](#cite_note-:02-29)
* 2022 – Brigadier General [Saifulrizal](/wiki/Saifulrizal_Abdul_Latif "Saifulrizal Abdul Latif") – Commander of the Royal Brunei Land Forces[[30]](#cite_note-30)
* 2022 – Sulaiman Alidin – Deputy Commissioner of Police[[31]](#cite_note-:6-31)
* 2022 – Brigadier General [Alirupendi](/wiki/Alirupendi "Alirupendi") – Joint Force Commander[[31]](#cite_note-:6-31)

### Second Class

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Pahlawan_Negara_Brunei&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Second Class")]

* 1963 – [Pengiran Jaya](/wiki/Pengiran_Jaya "Pengiran Jaya") – Commissioner of Police[[32]](#cite_note-:04-32)

### Third Class

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Pahlawan_Negara_Brunei&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Third Class")]

* Unknown – [Pengiran Mohammed Abdul Rahman Piut](/wiki/Pengiran_Mohammed_Abdul_Rahman_Piut "Pengiran Mohammed Abdul Rahman Piut") – Member of [Privy Council](/wiki/Privy_Council_(Brunei) "Privy Council (Brunei)")[[33]](#cite_note-33)
* 1963 – [Pengiran Mokhtar Puteh](/wiki/Pengiran_Mokhtar_Puteh "Pengiran Mokhtar Puteh") – Member of Privy Council[[34]](#cite_note-:062-34)
* 1963 – Ali Ghani – killed during [Brunei revolt](/wiki/Brunei_revolt "Brunei revolt") in 1962[[35]](#cite_note-35) (posthumous)[[36]](#cite_note-:10-36)

### Fourth Class

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Pahlawan_Negara_Brunei&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Fourth Class")]

* 1963 – Mohamed Tahir – killed during Brunei revolt in 1962[[37]](#cite_note-:11-37) (posthumous)[[36]](#cite_note-:10-36)
* 1963 – Selasa Othman – killed during Brunei revolt in 1962[[37]](#cite_note-:11-37) (posthumous)[[36]](#cite_note-:10-36)
